answer:
**Āṅgirasa Smṛti**, **Bṛhaspati Smṛti**, **Kātyāyana Smṛti**, **Manusmṛti**, and **Nārada Smṛti** offer prescriptive rules on marriage. These are ancient Sanskrit Dharmaśāstra texts that detail topics such as eligibility, timing, gotra restrictions, remarriage, duties of family members, women's property (*strīdhana*), and penalties for violations.[^1][^2][^3][^4][^5]

No other works in the corpus provide comparable systematic rules; passing mentions (e.g., in poetry like *Juliana* or economic treatises like *Nouveau traité d'économie*) are descriptive or incidental rather than regulatory.[^6]

[^1]: *Āṅgirasa Smṛti*, ch. 1 ([/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-b1e588](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-b1e588)), on remarriage of widows.
[^2]: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, ch. 1 ([/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-b9a5da](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-b9a5da)), on a father's duty to marry off his daughter timely.
[^3]: *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, ch. 75 ([/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/75/translation/#p-cfcc31](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/75/translation/#p-cfcc31)), on *strīdhana* given at marriage.
[^4]: *Manusmṛti*, ch. 3 ([/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-4355ac](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-4355ac)), on choosing a wife of one's own *varṇa*.
[^5]: *Nārada Smṛti*, ch. 1 ([/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-ed1ca0](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-ed1ca0)), on a maiden's right to choose a groom.
[^6]: E.g., *Juliana*, ch. 1, or *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, chs. 6–7.
